subject: Add protocol configuration to event publisher
commit_message:
Add protocol configuration to event publisher

The event publisher is now instantiated with the protocol
configuration property. This aligns the publisher with the
consumer, which already has that configuration capability.

Change-Id: I21173cc975cae84c7720557758a9c79f78eb2fd7
Issue-ID: AAI-1385
Signed-off-by: Michael Arrastia <MArrasti@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I'm not sure that replacing the externalized (XML-based) configuration beans files with a java based configuration is a good idea.  (This is more a comment around https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/56405/ - but unfortunately I was on vacation when that went in and didn't have a chance to comment).

The major advantage of using the event-client library is that we can easily switch between event bus technologies (DMaap, Kafka, RabbitMQ) simply by changing the configuration.

It seems that we now have introduced a build-time dependency.  For example, it appears that Spike is now hardwired to use DMaaP.  How would we switch to a kafka back-end?  

Ping me if you want to discuss.

Thanks,
Steve

Abandoned

Decided to preserve XML based configuration. Resolving issue with obfuscated topic passwords by adding deobfuscation to event-client.
